authErrors.user_already_registered: An account with this email already exists.
authErrors.email_not_confirmed: You need to confirm your email first. Check your inbox (including spam) and click the confirmation link.
authErrors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.

studentEmail.non_institutional_domain: Use your institutional university email (e.g. name.surname@students.youruni.edu).

HomePage.heading: You don't send résumés. Companies reach out to you.
HomePage.subhead: Chat with MIRA and your MiraCard takes shape: what you've actually done, who you are beyond the résumé — interests, how you work, where you want to go — and exactly what you're available for: which field, in which city, from when and for how long. No keyword filters: here you won't get dropped by an ATS for using the wrong term. The more you share, the more companies find you.
HomePage.ctaAssociations: And in the meantime you apply to your campus associations with the same profile.
HomePage.orgEyebrow: Are you a company or a university association?
HomePage.associationBody: Apply for your association: manage applications, board members, and your MIRA page.
HomePage.companyBody: Don't wait for applications. Find the right candidates: describe who you're looking for, MIRA shows you who really fits.

guide.headerIntro: Hi {name}! Let's build your MIRA Card.
guide.headerFilled: Got it — check your details and confirm.
guide.expImprove: Write it however it comes — I'll shape it.
guide.availability: What are you looking for, and what's next?
guide.skills: From your exams, here are your skills.
guide.profile: Last thing — who you are beyond the CV.

guide.building: Here's your cycle — positions, requirements, questions.
guide.detail: AI reads every profile — you decide.
Anonymous until they accept the contact.

LoginPage.heading: Log in
LoginPage.pendingRequest: Your access request is still awaiting confirmation from the MIRA team. You'll get an email as soon as it's approved.
LoginPage.wrongAccountType: This is a {accountType} account. Switch to "{targetLabel}" above to sign in.
LoginPage.callbackFailed: This confirmation link was already used — this often happens because some email filters open it automatically before you do. If you received the email, your address is likely already confirmed: try logging in below.

SignupPage.heading: Create your account
SignupPage.emailHelper: Use your university email, not a personal one.
SignupPage.passwordHelper: At least 8 characters, one uppercase letter, one number, and one special character.
SignupPage.selectUniversityAndDegree: Select your university and degree level.
SignupPage.emailSentBody: We've sent a confirmation link to {email}. Click the link to activate your account.
SignupPage.emailSentDelayNote: The email can take a few minutes to arrive — if you don't see it right away, check your spam folder too.
SignupPage.codeBody: We sent a 6-digit code to {email}. Enter it below to confirm your account.
SignupPage.codeResend: Didn't get it? Resend the code
SignupPage.codeResent: Code sent again — check your inbox.
SignupPage.codeInvalid: Invalid or expired code. Check it and try again.
SignupPage.termsConsent: I have read the <privacyLink>Privacy Policy</privacyLink> and I agree to the <termsLink>Terms of Service</termsLink>.

AziendePage.formIntro: Fill in your details to request access to MIRA. Our team will review the request and, if approved, send you an email to create your account.
AziendePage.contactEmailHelper: If the request is approved, you'll receive the account creation link at this address.

errors.duplicate_pending_request: You already have a pending request with this email.
errors.unknown: Something went wrong. Please try again.

landing.heading: Discover the right talent before anyone else, beyond the résumé
landing.subhead: A résumé tells you what a student has done. MIRA also shows you who they are, what they're looking for, when they're available, and how well they fit the role.
landing.pilotNote: Pilot launching September 2026. Free initial access.

step1.title: Describe what you're looking for
step1.body: Write in natural language the profile you need: role, skills, sector, attitude, availability, and much more.

step2.title: MIRA finds the matches
step2.body: The system compares your search with student profiles and shows you the most relevant candidates, with an explanation for each.

step3.title: Contact and interviews
step3.body: Send a contact request to the student. If they accept, a private chat opens where you can get to know each other and, if there's mutual interest, arrange an interview.

landing.whyHeading: Why this isn't just another recruiting portal

row1.left: You receive applications and résumés to sift through
row1.right: No job postings to publish every time.

row2.left: Information limited to what's stated in the résumé
row2.right: Profiles enriched with experience, interests, attitude, availability, and goals

row3.left: You don't really know what's behind a candidate
row3.right: Define who you want and automatically discover the best-fitting candidates.

row4.left: The student only updates their profile when job hunting
row4.right: Every match comes with an explanation of why it fits

StudentHome.completeCardCta: Complete your card: skills, languages and personal profile are missing (~5 minutes) →
StudentHome.autodescrizioneIntro: Written in your own words — feel free to edit it, it's the most personal part of the card.

CardDocument.empty: This student has no approved card blocks yet.
CardDocument.zoomIn: Tap the card to zoom in

CardBlocks.autodescrizionePlaceholder: Tell us who you are, in your own words...
CardBlocks.pianoCarrieraPlaceholder: How do you see yourself in the next 1-2 years?

header.mediaCambioNote: Only changes by re-uploading your transcript
header.reuploadTranscriptNote: Re-uploading refreshes exams and GPA from scratch based on the new transcript.

disponibilita.cosaCercaPlaceholder: e.g. curricular internship, part-time, not looking...
disponibilita.periodoPlaceholder: e.g. from September 2026, from June to August...
disponibilita.pianoPlaceholder: e.g. exchange, Master's, work, certifications... and the direction you have in mind

CardBlocks.profiloPersonalePlaceholder: How you present yourself beyond exams and experiences: real interests, habits, priorities...

JoinByCode.prompt: Are you a member of an association? Enter your invite code

JoinByCode.success: Request sent to {name}. They'll let you know once it's accepted.

Associazioni.pageSubtitle: Your applications and all associations on MIRA
Associazioni.noApplications: You don't have any applications yet. Find an association below.
Associazioni.noAssociationsAvailable: No associations available yet for your university.

Applications.manageFromAssociazioni: You can manage your activity in the association from the Associations section.

AziendeStudent.noRequestsBody: Companies will reach out once they find your profile interesting.
AziendeStudent.noChatsBody: Accept a request to open a chat with a company.

AziendeStudent.shareDialogBody: Choose what to share with the company. You can share just one field, and do it again later to add more.

AssociationsListing.heading: Associations on MIRA
AssociationsListing.subhead: Discover the university associations active on MIRA and apply directly from their page.

ApplicationForm.positionQuestionLabel: Which position are you applying for?

CandidatiPage.registrationErrorGeneric: Error during registration. Please try again.

CandidatiPage.heading: Apply for your association
CandidatiPage.intro: Fill in your association's details and create your president account. Your request will be verified within 24h. You'll receive a confirmation email. Meanwhile, you can already start building your student profile.
CandidatiPage.descriptionPlaceholder: What the association does, in a couple of lines...
CandidatiPage.credentialsSectionIntro: These details also create your MIRA student profile: you can start building your MiraCard right away, while your association is being reviewed.
CandidatiPage.loginSectionIntro: Use your existing MIRA account credentials: the application will be linked to your profile.
CandidatiPage.switchToLogin: Already have an account? Log in
CandidatiPage.switchToCreate: Don't have an account? Sign up
CandidatiPage.passwordHelper: At least 8 characters, one uppercase letter, one number, and one special character.

InAttesaPage.heading: Application pending
InAttesaPage.body: We've received your association's application. We'll review it as soon as possible and you'll receive an email with the outcome.
InAttesaPage.meanwhileBody: Start building your student profile on MIRA — your MiraCard. You don't need to wait for the association's approval to do this.

steps.step4: You log in and start managing applications and the board

InAttesaPage.footer: For questions, reply to the confirmation email you'll receive.

AssociationLayout.onboardingBanner: Before continuing, complete your MiraCard profile (~5 minutes) →
AssociationLayout.pendingBody: We'll review your association as soon as possible and email you the outcome. You'll get access to the dashboard once it's approved.
AssociationLayout.publicPageBanner: Until you publish your public page, candidates can't find you.

GuidedHome.intro: Hi! I'm MIRA. Let's build {name}'s presence on MIRA together, one step at a time. I'll walk you through it: finish one step and we move to the next. You can skip anything and come back later.
GuidedHome.introDone: All set, {name}! Your presence on MIRA is complete. From here you can always update the page, manage members and open new application cycles.
GuidedHome.someSkipped: You skipped some steps: you can resume them anytime from here.

public_page.title: Build your public page
public_page.body: It's the showcase students see when they look up your association. It's built like a card.

collaborators.title: Invite collaborators
collaborators.body: The other board people who'll run the dashboard alongside you.

members.title: Invite members
members.body: Add your association's members: manage them here, each with their MiraCard.

cycles.title: Set up applications
cycles.body: Create your first application cycle: MIRA guides you through building it.

Board.heading: Members
Board.subhead: Who works with you and who belongs to the association
Board.collaboratorsBody: Anyone joining with your invite link will see the same dashboard you do: they can manage application cycles, candidates and the public page, exactly like you.
Board.inviteStep2: 2. Send it to whoever you want to bring in
Board.inviteStep3: 3. When they use it, you get a request to approve below
Board.regenerateHint: The previous link will stop working.
Board.membershipTitle: Do you also want to manage the association's members?
Board.membershipBodyOff: Turning on membership management lets you bring in all the association's members, not just those helping with the dashboard. You can split them into sections you create yourself (Marketing, M&A, Events...), see each one's degree and level, and open their MiraCard. Members do NOT see the dashboard: they only live in your list. Whenever you want, you can make any of them an admin.
Board.membershipBodyOn: Membership management is on. Anyone joining with the link becomes a member of the association and does not see the dashboard. Sort them into your sections below, and promote them to admin if you need to.
Board.noPeopleHint: Share the invite link above to bring someone in.
Board.sectionsHint: Create your association's sections and put everyone in the right one from the dropdown.
Board.deleteSectionConfirm: Delete this section? Everyone in it goes back to "No section".
Board.noCard: This person doesn't have a MiraCard yet.
Board.guideMembers: Now invite your members and manage them from here. Share the link below: whoever joins appears among the association's people, each with their MiraCard. You can appoint other admins or give each one a role.

InviteCodeSection.heading: Invite link
InviteCodeSection.description: Share this link to invite people to the board. Whoever uses it sends a join request you can approve below.

CycleCard.startIntro: Let's build your application cycle together. I'll ask one question at a time and you'll watch the card fill in below: six blocks, then you decide when to open applications.
CycleCard.startError: The cycle could not be created. Please try again.
CycleCard.candidateViewHint: You're seeing the cycle the way a student sees it.
CycleCard.boardViewHint: Full view: includes what students don't see.
CycleCard.noQuestionsLabel: We don't want to ask specific questions
CycleCard.readyToPublish: The card is complete. Once you open the cycle, students can see it and apply.

guide.nome: Let's start with the name. What do you want to call this {association} cycle? Students see it too: the type of selection and the period usually do the job, like “Fall 2026 Recruiting”.
guide.descrizione: Now write what a student should know before applying: who you are, what you're looking for in this cycle, how the selection works. You can leave it empty and move on.
guide.date: When do you open and when do you close? On the closing date the cycle closes by itself and no one can apply anymore.
guide.posizioni: Are you opening for specific positions, or is this open to everyone? If you have defined roles, list them: MIRA can suggest the best fit to each candidate.
guide.profilo: This is the most important block, and only you can see it. Describe your ideal candidate: interests, skills, year of study, useful experience, attitude. MIRA uses this to evaluate applicants.
guide.domande: Last block: do you want to ask candidates specific questions? Their answers arrive together with their MIRA Card. If you don't need any, tick the box and we're done.

visibility.internal: For your eyes only: students don't see this.

placeholders.descrizione: What a student should know before applying...
placeholders.profilo: Interests, skills, year of study, useful experience, attitude...
placeholders.question: Write the question...

NewCycleWizard.initialMessage: Let's create the new application cycle together! What do you want to call it? (e.g. "Recruiting Fall 2026")
NewCycleWizard.heading: New application cycle
NewCycleWizard.subhead: MIRA will guide you through creating the cycle. Answer the questions one at a time.

CycleDetail.customQuestionsSubhead: These questions will be shown to candidates during their application.

CycleEditor.aiCriteriaHelper: Describe the ideal candidate: interests, skills, attitude, year of study, useful experience. MIRA will use these criteria to evaluate all candidates and suggest the best-fitting position. Not visible to candidates.
CycleEditor.aiCriteriaPlaceholder: e.g. We're looking for second- or third-year students interested in finance and markets. We prefer profiles with leadership experience, demonstrated curiosity, and availability of at least 6 hours a week.

QuestionBuilder.questionTextPlaceholder: e.g. Why do you want to join our association?

CandidateDetail.noEvidence: Not enough evidence on this cycle's criteria.

CandidateActions.editBeforeSending: Edit the message before sending it. The email will be sent by MIRA on your behalf.

RegenerateEvaluationButton.notAvailable: The AI evaluation isn't available yet.

InterviewsPage.heading: Interviews
InterviewsPage.noInterviews: No interviews scheduled. Schedule candidates from their detail page.

PublicPageEditor.heading: Public page
PublicPageEditor.subhead: Edit the information visible on the association's public page
PublicPageEditor.logoHelper: PNG, JPG — max 2MB. If not uploaded, the initial letter is used.

PageCard.heading: Your public page
PageCard.previewHint: You're seeing the page the way a student sees it.
PageCard.boardHint: The page students see when they look up your association.
PageCard.readyToPublish: The page is ready. Once published, students can find you and apply to your cycles.
PageCard.publishedInfo: The page is published and visible to students.
PageCard.logoHelper: PNG, JPG or WEBP. Square, ideally at least 200×200.
PageCard.sectorsHelper: Separate them with a comma. E.g. Finance, Consulting, Marketing

guide.identita: Let's start with {name}'s identity: upload the logo, check the name and pick a category. It's the first thing a student sees.
guide.descrizione: Tell them who you are. The short description is the line that shows first; the full one is where you explain what you do, your projects, what it's like to join.
guide.settori: Which sectors or areas represent you? They help students grasp at a glance what you're about. If you don't have any, you can skip.
guide.contatti: Last block: where can they find you? A website and a contact email. You can leave them empty and add them later.

placeholders.long: Who you are, what you do, your projects, what it's like to be part of it...

CompanyProfile.heading: Company profile
CompanyProfile.descriptionPlaceholder: Briefly describe the company, what you do, the culture…

CompanyContacts.noContactsTitle: No contacts yet. Use search to find candidates and contact them.
CompanyContacts.selectContactPrompt: Select a contact with an open chat to write.
CompanyContacts.chatOpensHint: The chat opens once the student accepts your request.
CompanyContacts.locationPlaceholder: E.g. Milan office, via Dante 1 / Google Meet
CompanyContacts.notesPlaceholder: E.g. Bring an ID, 45-min technical interview…

ContactRequestModal.sentBody: The student will receive a notification.

ContactRequestModal.intro: The student will see your company's name and the message. They can accept or decline.
ContactRequestModal.messagePlaceholder: Briefly introduce yourself and explain why you're contacting this candidate…

CompanySearch.noSearches: No searches yet. Start a new one.
CompanySearch.assistantIntro: Describe the profile you're looking for — role, skills, sector, attitude, availability. I can also ask questions to better understand what you need.
CompanySearch.searchPlaceholder: Describe the profile you're looking for...

PendingPage.step2: If approved, you'll get an email with the link to create your account

AdminCompanies.invitationCreatedEmailFailed: Invitation created, but the email failed to send. Copy the link and send it manually:

AdminAssociations.heading: Associations
AdminAssociations.subhead: Pending applications, direct invites, and active associations on MIRA
AdminAssociations.reminderSubjectDefault: Complete your association page on MIRA
AdminAssociations.reminderMessageDefault:
Hi,

{name}'s request has been approved on MIRA, but the association's public page hasn't been completed and published yet. Complete it to make it visible to students and start receiving applications.

Manage it now using the button below.

See you soon,
The MIRA team
AdminAssociations.inviteIntro: They'll immediately receive an email from MIRA with the link to create their account and the association's page.
AdminAssociations.emailMessagePlaceholder: What they need to know or do — this text will appear in the email they receive.
AdminAssociations.invitationCreatedEmailFailed: Invitation created, but the email failed to send. Copy the link and send it manually:
AdminAssociations.invitationCreatedEmailSent: Invitation created and sent via email. Invite link:
AdminAssociations.deleteConfirmPrompt:
This action permanently deletes "{name}" and all its data (applications, board, cycles, etc). It cannot be undone.

Type {word} to confirm.

AdminUsers.heading: Users
AdminUsers.signupLinkIntro: Share this link to have new students register on MIRA (requires an institutional university email).
AdminUsers.deleteConfirmPrompt:
This action permanently deletes the account of "{name}" and all its data (applications, associations, chats, etc). It cannot be undone.

Type {word} to confirm.
AdminUsers.reminderMessageDefault:
Hi {name},

you started signing up on MIRA but haven't completed your MIRA Card yet. It only takes a few minutes, and your card becomes visible to associations and companies on the platform.

Complete it now using the button below.

See you soon,
The MIRA team

OnboardingFlow.transcriptReadError: I couldn't read the file — {error}. You can retry or skip.
OnboardingFlow.cvReadError: I couldn't fully read your CV: check the experiences below and fix them by hand.
OnboardingFlow.hintBocconi: You can download it from you@b: in the certificates/self-certifications section you'll find the self-certification with your exam list.
OnboardingFlow.hintGeneric: You can usually find it on your university's website or student portal, under documents and certificates.
OnboardingFlow.guideHeaderIntro:
Hi {name}! I'm MIRA: let's build your MIRA Card together — you need it to apply to associations, and it's the profile companies will use to find and contact you. It will be written in English, I'll take care of the translation.

Let's start with your studies: upload your transcript as a PDF and I'll fill in the fields below, exams, credits and average included. {hint} If you don't have it handy, skip and fill them in by hand.
OnboardingFlow.guideHeaderAfterUpload: Done: I read {courses} exams, {credits} credits{avg}. Check the fields below — fix anything you want, then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideHeaderUploaded: Your transcript is already uploaded: check the fields below, fix anything you want, then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideHeaderManual: No problem: fill in the fields below — program and years are enough, you can upload the transcript anytime from your Profile. Then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideEsperienze: Now your experiences. Do you have a CV? Upload it and I'll prepare them in English — then you review and fix them. If you don't have one, add them by hand below: personal projects, competitions, competitive sports, volunteering and student association activities all count — anything you think represents you. Write descriptions however they come: ✦ Improve with MIRA rewrites them in CV-style English.
OnboardingFlow.guideEsperienzeManual: Alright, let's build them by hand: add your experiences below. Personal projects, competitions, competitive sports, volunteering and student association activities all count. Write descriptions however they come — ✦ Improve with MIRA rewrites them in CV-style English. Then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideEsperienzeAfterCV: I found {count, plural, =0 {your experiences} one {1 experience} other {# experiences}} in your CV and prepared them below in English. Review them, fix them or add more — including the ones you'd never put on a CV (projects, sports, volunteering, association activities). Then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideDisponibilita: Last thing to unlock applications: let's define your availability and next steps. Use the toggle to tell me if you're looking or open to opportunities, and fill in the fields. Below, tell me your plans for the coming months/years (exchange, graduation, Master's, work, certifications...) and whether you already have a clear direction or are still exploring — that's perfectly fine too. Write the way you speak: ✦ Improve with MIRA turns it into proper English. Then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideCompetenzeProposed: From your exams I've already proposed some academic skills below — confirm, fix or remove them. Then add your hard skills: tools, software, methodologies or practical abilities you can really use (if I found any in your CV, they're already in). When everything looks right, press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideCompetenzeManual: Now your skills. Academic: what you've studied and acquired in theory (e.g. financial statement analysis, accounting, statistics, marketing). Hard: tools and practical abilities you can use (e.g. Excel, Python). Add them below in English — that's the card's format. Then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideLingue: Which languages do you know? Add them below with level and any certification (e.g. English — C1 — IELTS 7.5). Then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideLingueWithCV: I've already added the languages found in your CV below: check levels and certifications, add any missing ones, then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.guideProfilo:
Now the part that's usually missing from CVs: how you present yourself beyond exams and experiences. No hobby lists, no interview-style lines — tell me something authentic:
— What do you follow on your own, outside your course syllabi?
— Outside your studies, what do you do consistently?
— How would people who know you well describe you?
— What can't you stand at work or in your studies, or what would you never give up?
— What's the most "you" thing you've done in the last year?
Write the way you speak, Italian is fine too: ✦ Improve with MIRA turns it into a first-person text in English. Then press Confirm.
OnboardingFlow.gateBody: Your card is at {pct}%: you can already apply to associations on MIRA. Or we can complete the last sections in a few minutes.
OnboardingFlow.gateWarning: NOTE: The more complete your profile, the more you attract companies' attention!
OnboardingFlow.finalBody: You'll find it in the Profile section, and you can edit it anytime. Good luck with your applications.
